response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (founding GISCollective, building GIS applications for Green Map) and proven low-latency systems work handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic using DLang with async I/O and fibers. However, their C++ experience is dated (2014-2015 at National Instruments) and they have no Rust, while the role requires modern C++ as the primary skill. Their recent work has shifted heavily toward TypeScript/Node full-stack product development, and they lack direct experience with routing engines, navigation guidance, or tile generation pipelines. The systems fundamentals and geodata background make them worth a conversation, but the core language and navigation-domain gaps are significant.
strengths: Deep geospatial data experience via GISCollective founder role and Green Map GIS applications
Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work (millions of HTTP req/sec, async I/O, caching) in DLang
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, Linux and distributed microservices experience
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; no Rust experience listed
No direct routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile pipeline experience
Recent years focused on TypeScript/Node web product work, away from systems programming
